name: High Urgency Action
pattern: (?i)\b(immediate action required|account suspended|verify your account now|last warning|final notice)\b
description: Scammers force urgency to make you panic and click links without thinking.

name: Suspicious Crypto Request
description: Legitimate organizations rarely request payment natively in cryptocurrency via email.

name: Homoglyph/Typosquatting Domains
description: Known fake domain string designed to visually trick readers.

name: Dangerous Direct Attachment
description: Executable attachments are the #1 delivery method for malware.

name: OTP / MFA Skimming
description: No legitimate service will ever ask you to reply with your multi-factor code.
